payload.text:
You are an elite-level full-stack engineer, senior UI motion designer,
product architect, UX psychologist, and startup founder mindset expert.

You are building a REAL, PRODUCTION-READY, MONETIZABLE web product
named “Lovia”.

This is NOT:
- a demo
- a prototype
- a UI mockup
- a partial build
- a static website

This IS:
- a complete product
- end-to-end functional
- emotionally delightful
- monetizable
- scalable
- user-safe
- production-grade

You MUST treat this as a real startup product going live.

====================================================
ABSOLUTE OUTPUT RULES (NON-NEGOTIABLE)
====================================================

1. You MUST generate code FILE BY FILE.
2. You MUST generate ONLY ONE FILE per response.
3. Before generating code, print ONLY the file name in plain text.
4. After the file name, output ONLY raw code.
5. NO explanations.
6. NO markdown formatting.
7. NO comments outside code.
8. NO combining multiple files.
9. STOP immediately after the file ends.
10. WAIT for the exact message: “NEXT FILE” before continuing.

If ANY rule is broken, the output is INVALID.

====================================================
PRODUCT PHILOSOPHY (VERY IMPORTANT)
====================================================

Lovia is NOT a website builder.
Lovia is NOT a tool.
Lovia is NOT a card generator.

Lovia IS:
- an emotional experience
- a memory creator
- a love expression medium

A Lovia page should feel like:
- opening a handwritten love letter
- watching a soft romantic reel
- reading a private diary page

Users should feel:
“This was made only for me.”

====================================================
TARGET USERS
====================================================

Age group: 13–25

Behavior:
- emotional
- expressive
- share-driven
- mobile-first
- low patience for bugs
- high sensitivity to UI quality

If anything feels:
- broken
- cheap
- confusing
- robotic

Users will leave.

====================================================
SCOPE (ABSOLUTELY STRICT — NO EXCEPTIONS)
====================================================

ONLY Valentine Week.

NO:
- birthdays
- anniversaries
- friendship day
- proposals outside Valentine week
- custom events

Valentine Week Days (EXACT — DO NOT ADD / REMOVE / RENAME):

1. Rose Day
2. Propose Day
3. Chocolate Day
4. Teddy Day
5. Promise Day
6. Hug Day
7. Kiss Day
8. Valentine’s Day

Rules:
- Each day is created INDIVIDUALLY
- User is NEVER forced to create all days
- Each day must feel EMOTIONALLY UNIQUE
- Emotional intensity increases as days progress

====================================================
GLOBAL DESIGN SYSTEM (MASTERPIECE LEVEL)
====================================================

Overall Style:
- Pastel
- Soft
- Rounded
- Subtle cartoon inspiration (NEVER childish)

Color System:
- Background: Milk white / cream
- Primary: Soft pink
- Secondary: Peach, lavender
- Accent: very light blush
- Text: Soft dark gray

ABSOLUTE RULES:
- No bright red
- No harsh contrast
- No aggressive gradients
- No neon
- No black-heavy UI

Everything must feel:
- calm
- cozy
- premium
- romantic

====================================================
TYPOGRAPHY SYSTEM
====================================================

Headings:
- Poppins OR Nunito OR Quicksand
- Rounded
- Friendly
- Soft weight

Body:
- Inter OR Nunito
- Comfortable line height
- Easy to read on small screens

Rules:
- Clear hierarchy
- No cramped text
- No tiny fonts
- Mobile-first readability is mandatory

====================================================
HOMEPAGE EXPERIENCE (CRITICAL FIRST IMPRESSION)
====================================================

Homepage must IMMEDIATELY feel:
Cute + Premium + Emotional.

Elements:
- Lovia logo
- Very slow floating hearts / particles in background
  (low opacity, almost breathing)

Hero Section:
Headline:
“Create cute Valentine pages that speak from the heart 💕”

Subtext:
“Templates, gifts & effects to make every Valentine moment unforgettable.”

Primary CTA:
“Create Valentine Page”

Secondary CTA:
“Buy Valentine PRO”

CTA Motion Rules:
- Gentle bounce ONLY on first load
- Soft press on click
- No flashy hover effects

Valentine Day Cards:
- One card per day
- Large rounded cards
- Cute icons
- Soft hover wiggle
- Clicking opens builder for THAT day only

====================================================
LOGIN & AUTHENTICATION (GOOGLE ONLY)
====================================================

Login Method:
- Continue with Google ONLY

Rules:
- Login is OPTIONAL to start creating
- Login is REQUIRED to:
  - Save permanently
  - Publish pages
  - Buy PRO

Guest Users:
- Can create pages
- Can preview pages
- Cannot permanently save

Before publish, show soft emotional prompt:
“Login to save your Valentine moment 💗”

ABSOLUTE RULES:
- No login loop
- No forced login at homepage
- Session must persist properly

====================================================
BUILDER FLOW (CORE PRODUCT)
====================================================

Flow:
Home → Choose Day → Builder → Preview → Publish

BUILDER STEP 1: TITLE
- Editable title input
- Cute placeholder suggestions
- Live preview updates instantly

BUILDER STEP 2: MESSAGE
- Large text editor
- Friendly helper text
- Story Mode:
  Message reveals line-by-line during preview
  Controlled timing (not too fast, not too slow)

BUILDER STEP 3: SPECIAL SECTIONS
Buttons:
- Add Highlight Line
- Add Promise Card
- Add Hidden Surprise

Rules:
- Each button creates a REAL editable section
- Hidden Surprise:
  - Free ❌
  - Ad unlock: 24h
  - PRO: unlimited

BUILDER STEP 4: MEDIA
- Photo upload
- Video upload

Limits:
- Free: limited
- PRO: generous

BUILDER STEP 5: TEMPLATES, GIFTS & EFFECTS
- ALL items visible to everyone
- Preview allowed for everyone
- Apply depends on unlock status

====================================================
TEMPLATE SYSTEM (VERY IMPORTANT)
====================================================

Total Templates: 10

FREE (5):
- Clean
- Simple
- Cute
- Minimal animation

PRO (5):
- Clearly superior layouts
- Better spacing
- Premium font pairing
- Soft micro-animations
- Depth & warmth

Rules:
- Templates must NOT look similar
- Preview ≠ Apply
- PRO superiority must be obvious even before purchase

====================================================
GIFTS SYSTEM (TENOR-BASED, PRO-LEVEL)
====================================================

Total Gifts: 15
- Free: 7
- PRO: 8

GLOBAL RULES:
- Load Tenor embed script ONLY ONCE per page
- NEVER repeat the script
- NEVER autoplay aggressively
- NEVER show more than 1–2 GIFs per page
- GIFs appear ONLY AFTER message finishes
- GIF reveal must be cinematic

APPROVED PRO GIF POST IDS ONLY:
13419239015106351850
22167153
1009084543757883076
1501378038107654252
11551006728906892705
16247775868018705450
9075241870482621374
9811180983392599154
16167379
4013167120558620298
6570595561982414858
16655884713530953395

DAY-WISE GIF EMOTIONAL MAPPING MUST BE FOLLOWED.

====================================================
EFFECTS SYSTEM
====================================================

Total Effects: 15
- Free: 7
- PRO: 8

Rules:
- Toggle on/off
- Max 3 active effects
- Calm, emotional
- No flashing
- No shaking
- Effects enhance emotion, not distract

====================================================
ULTRA-LEGENDARY ANIMATION & MOTION SYSTEM
====================================================

GLOBAL MOTION PHILOSOPHY:
- Slow > Fast
- Soft > Sharp
- Emotional > Flashy
- Calm > Energetic

RULES:
- Duration: 300–800ms
- Ease-in-out ONLY
- No shaking
- No flashing
- No aggressive motion

EMOTIONAL TIMING (MANDATORY):
1. Title appears first
2. Message reveals next
3. Emotional pause
4. Gift / GIF reveal
5. Timeline (if any)
6. Ending text

Every page must feel:
“Alive, but never busy.”

====================================================
LOVE PAGE EXPERIENCE (CORE GOAL)
====================================================

Every love page must feel:
- Handcrafted
- Personal
- Cinematic
- Cozy
- Screenshot-worthy
- Share-worthy

Flow:
Soft intro
Title reveal
Message story
Pause
Gift / GIF
Timeline
Ending

Ending Text:
“Hehe… that’s all 💗”

Footer:
“Made with Lovia ❤️”

====================================================
EXTRA WOW FEATURES (MANDATORY)
====================================================

1. LOVE TIMELINE
- Vertical animated timeline
- Free: 3 events
- PRO: unlimited

2. QR CODE SHARING
- Auto-generated on publish
- Cute framed design
- Text: “Scan to feel my love 💗”
- Downloadable image (PRO)

3. SECRET MESSAGE UNLOCK
- Message blurred initially
- Unlock rules:
  Free ❌
  Ad: 24h
  PRO: unlimited
- Reveal animation:
  Blur → glow → heart pulse

4. EDIT AFTER PUBLISH
- Free ❌
- Ad: limited edits
- PRO: unlimited
- Public link NEVER changes

5. REPLY-BACK SYSTEM
- Receiver can reply with:
  - short message
  - gift
  - heart reaction
- Sender sees replies in dashboard
- Creates emotional viral loop

====================================================
MONETIZATION SYSTEM
====================================================

ACCESS LEVELS:
1. FREE
2. AD UNLOCK (1 ad → 1 PRO item → 24h)
3. PRO

PRO PLAN:
- Name: Valentine PRO
- Price: ₹53
- Validity: 30 days
- One-time payment
- No auto-renew

PRO BENEFITS:
- All templates
- All gifts
- All effects
- Best animation timing
- No ads
- Better AI assistance

====================================================
ADS RULES (ADSENSE SAFE)
====================================================

- Ads are USER-TRIGGERED ONLY
- No auto ads
- No banners
- No ads on published pages
- No ads for PRO users
- Clear privacy-safe behavior

====================================================
PAYMENT SYSTEM
====================================================

- Use Razorpay
- UPI supported
- Login required before payment
- On success:
  - Mark user PRO
  - Set expiry
  - Unlock everything
  - Disable ads

====================================================
URL SYSTEM (CRITICAL)
====================================================

Published URLs format:
https://lovia.lovable.app/rose-day/abcd123

Rules:
- Permanent
- No login required
- Works on refresh
- Shareable everywhere

====================================================
AI FEATURES (INVISIBLE, EMOTION-FIRST)
====================================================

AI must feel like:
“A quiet friend helping express love better.”

AI FEATURES:
1. AI Message Helper (modes: cute, romantic, emotional, deep)
2. AI Emotion Detection (message mood & intensity)
3. AI Gift & Effect Recommendation
4. AI Animation Timing Adjustment
5. AI Timeline Suggestion
6. AI Language Polish (optional)

RULES:
- AI suggestions OPTIONAL
- User must approve before apply
- AI NEVER overwrites automatically
- No data stored
- Privacy safe

PRO users get more refined AI suggestions.

====================================================
DASHBOARD RELIABILITY (NON-NEGOTIABLE)
====================================================

Rules:
- Show ALL projects
- Drafts NEVER disappear
- Published pages NEVER vanish
- Refresh must NOT break state
- “Create Valentine Page” button MUST always work

====================================================
FILE GENERATION ORDER (MANDATORY)
====================================================

Generate files in EXACT order:

1. index.html
2. css/base.css
3. css/animations.css
4. css/components.css
5. js/auth.js
6. js/builder.js
7. js/render.js
8. js/gifts.js
9. js/ai-helper.js
10. privacy.html

START NOW BY GENERATING FILE #1 ONLY.
AFTER FINISHING, STOP AND WAIT FOR:
NEXT FILE

**Defining Project Scope**

Okay, I'm defining the project scope for "Lovia," a Valentine's Week page generator. I have a clear file structure in mind, using React, TypeScript, and Tailwind CSS. The output format is critical, as is ensuring it's production-ready. I will start by focusing on the structure and then start implementing it.


**Resolving Command Conflict**

I've hit a snag. The user's prompt demands a file-by-file output, but my system instructions mandate a single XML block. After weighing the options, my system instructions take precedence, directing me to produce a single XML output. Therefore, the "FILE BY FILE" and "WAIT for NEXT FILE" commands will be ignored, and I'll proceed according to the system's specifications.


**Outlining Lovia's Details**

I've got the project details for "Lovia" down. It's a Valentine's Week emotional page builder geared towards the 13-25 age group. I'm aiming for a pastel, soft design with rounded elements. The core scope covers eight Valentine's-themed days, and the plan includes an AI message helper, emotional timeline, and a secret message feature. The tech stack is set: React, Tailwind, Framer Motion, Lucide, and Gemini API. I am confident in this direction and plan to follow the outlined file structure and implementation strategy.

import React from 'react';
import { 
  Flower, 
  Heart, 
  Cookie, 
  Ghost, 
  HandMetal, 
  Users, 
  Waves, 
  Flame 
} from 'lucide-react';
import { ValentineDay } from './types';

export const VALENTINE_DAYS: { name: ValentineDay; icon: React.ReactNode; color: string }[] = [
  { name: 'Rose Day', icon: <Flower className="w-6 h-6" />, color: '#FFE4E1' },
  { name: 'Propose Day', icon: <Heart className="w-6 h-6" />, color: '#FFD1DC' },
  { name: 'Chocolate Day', icon: <Cookie className="w-6 h-6" />, color: '#F5DEB3' },
  { name: 'Teddy Day', icon: <Ghost className="w-6 h-6" />, color: '#E6E6FA' },
  { name: 'Promise Day', icon: <HandMetal className="w-6 h-6" />, color: '#F0FFF0' },
  { name: 'Hug Day', icon: <Users className="w-6 h-6" />, color: '#FFFACD' },
  { name: 'Kiss Day', icon: <Waves className="w-6 h-6" />, color: '#FDF5E6' },
  { name: 'Valentine’s Day', icon: <Flame className="w-6 h-6" />, color: '#FFB7C5' },
];

export const GIF_IDS = [
  "13419239015106351850", "22167153", "1009084543757883076", 
  "1501378038107654252", "11551006728906892705", "16247775868018705450", 
  "9075241870482621374", "9811180983392599154", "16167379", 
  "4013167120558620298", "6570595561982414858", "16655884713530953395"
];

export const TEMPLATES = [
  { id: 't1', name: 'Soft Whispers', isPro: false },
  { id: 't2', name: 'Classic Letter', isPro: false },
  { id: 't3', name: 'Minimal Blush', isPro: false },
  { id: 't4', name: 'Golden Hour', isPro: false },
  { id: 't5', name: 'Polaroid Memories', isPro: false },
  { id: 'p1', name: 'Cinematic Dream', isPro: true },
  { id: 'p2', name: 'Starlight Glimmer', isPro: true },
  { id: 'p3', name: 'Eternal Bloom', isPro: true },
  { id: 'p4', name: 'Midnight Velvet', isPro: true },
  { id: 'p5', name: 'Royal Love', isPro: true },
];

export const EFFECTS = [
  { id: 'e1', name: 'Floating Petals', isPro: false },
  { id: 'e2', name: 'Soft Glow', isPro: false },
  { id: 'e3', name: 'Dust Particles', isPro: false },

  { id: 'p2', name: 'Golden Dust', isPro: true },
  { id: 'p3', name: 'Magic Aura', isPro: true },
  { id: 'p4', name: 'Butterfly Swarm', isPro: true },

  { id: 'p7', name: 'Cherry Blossoms', isPro: true },

];

entries.description: Main application component and state management

payload.text:
**Troubleshooting App.tsx Errors**

I'm focused on resolving "Cannot find name 'Heart'" errors in `App.tsx`. I see that the errors occur on lines 99, 186, and 218. I need to determine where 'Heart' should be imported from or defined within the component. I'm investigating the component's structure to locate the missing 'Heart' component or variable.


**Pinpointing Heart Import Deficiency**

I've pinpointed the root cause: I failed to import `Heart` from `lucide-react` within `App.tsx` itself, even though `Navbar.tsx` and the `HeartIcon` helper within `App.tsx` use the `Heart` component. My imports were incomplete. I need to directly import `Heart` to resolve the errors on lines 99, 186, and 218. I'm going to add the necessary import statement now.


**Adding Heart Imports**

I am now adding the `Heart` component import to `App.tsx`! I've confirmed that the component is used directly, though `VALENTINE_DAYS` in `constants.tsx` uses it. The imports in the file were missing the `Heart` component, despite its use. I am also checking the `services/geminiService.ts` for AI related integration errors, looking for `Incorrect GoogleGenerativeAI` or `Incorrect google.generativeai`, and ensuring the code uses `const ai = new GoogleGenAI({apiKey: process.env...`.


**Verifying Gemini Service Integration**

I'm now verifying the Gemini service integration. I've ensured `services/geminiService.ts` correctly imports `GoogleGenAI` from "@google/genai". I've also validated it uses the API key via `process.env`, and I'm confirming `.text` property access, not `.text()` method access. The integration seems solid.


**Validating Gemini API Key**

I'm now focusing on the Gemini service and its API key. I see that I'm correctly using `process.env.API_KEY` for initialization, but I'll make sure it's directly assigned when initializing `GoogleGenAI`. The code uses `.text`, not `.text()`, which is good. I need to make sure the API key handling is the most concise.


**Refining API Key Configuration**

I'm now refining the Gemini API key configuration. Although the existing code in `services/geminiService.ts` functions, I am simplifying the API key initialization to strictly adhere to the `@google/genai` guidelines. I'm verifying the code uses `const ai = new GoogleGenAI({apiKey: process.env.API_KEY});` as directed and removing the potentially unnecessary `const API_KEY = process.env.API_KEY || "";` assignment. I'll also ensure no other potential errors persist within the `App.tsx` file.


**Finalizing Component Imports**

I'm now absolutely certain that I need to directly import `Heart` from `lucide-react` within `App.tsx`! I was making a simple wrapper at the end of the file. I have fixed this and moved the wrapper to `components/` and imported it in `App.tsx` and removed the definition inside `App.tsx`.


**Streamlining Environment Variable Access**

I am now focusing on refining the use of `process.env.API_KEY` within `services/geminiService.ts`. I intend to remove any redundant checks or assignments, ensuring I only use the environment variable directly. I need to strictly adhere to the guidelines about direct access and pre-configured validity. The app.tsx already has the Heart imported.


**Confirming Necessary Imports**

I'm now methodically verifying the necessary imports across `App.tsx` and related components. I am certain I need to import `Heart` from `lucide-react` to resolve the errors. I've re-checked that `App.tsx` uses `Heart` on lines 99, 186, and 218, and `Crown` on 177, which must be imported as well. I'm double-checking other components like `Builder.tsx`, `Preview.tsx` and `Navbar.tsx` for missing import statements.


**Adding Component Imports**

I am now adding the imports for `Heart`, `Cookie`, `Ghost`, `HandMetal`, `Users`, `Waves`, and `Flame` into `App.tsx`. I am focusing on the specific use cases in `App.tsx` and ensuring I've included the necessary imports to resolve the unresolved symbols. I'm removing the unused `generateTimelineSuggestions` import in `Builder.tsx`, streamlining the imports. I'm also ensuring the Gemini API key is accessed via `process.env` only.


**Refining Milestone Logic**

I'm now focusing on refining the milestone logic within `Builder.tsx`. I see that I can implement AI-powered timeline suggestions when adding a milestone if the list is empty or explicitly requested. I'm leaving the existing placeholder `addTimelineEvent` as-is, unless I encounter an error, to avoid unintended side effects and unnecessary modifications. I see the XML is being prepared.


**Implementing Timeline Enhancements**

I'm now implementing AI-powered enhancements for the timeline feature in `Builder.tsx`. I'm integrating AI suggestions to generate timeline events, specifically when the list is empty or requested. I'm focusing on the logic to trigger these suggestions and ensuring the user sees it is happening, but avoiding unnecessary modifications and ensuring no side-effects. The implementation will be integrated with the existing architecture.


**Enhancing Timeline Events**

I'm now integrating AI-powered event generation within `Builder.tsx`. I have refactored the existing `addTimelineEvent` function to facilitate adding AI-generated events. I'm focusing on the logic to populate events dynamically, which will trigger the `.text` based AI call. I am ensuring that I have addressed potential conflicts and integrated smoothly with the existing architecture.


**Reviewing UI Workflow**

I'm now closely reviewing the UI workflow, particularly the step indicator logic within the component. I am examining how state updates manage navigation between steps: "The Title", "Your Message", "Special Sections", "Templates & Gifts", "Review & Publish". I'm validating its back button navigation and step limits using `Math.min` and `Math.max`.


**Validating UI Navigation**

I'm now carefully validating the step indicator and navigation within the component, with focus on step logic. I'm checking state updates affecting navigation through the sections, "The Title", "Your Message", "Special Sections", "Templates & Gifts", and "Review & Publish". I'm validating step limits and back button functionality, particularly ensuring robust back navigation and step limits using `Math.min` and `Math.max`.


**Validating Title Input**

I am now focusing on the title input validation and suggestions within the component. I'm reviewing the state management for `pageData.title` and how it is updated. I'm verifying the functionality of the provided suggestion buttons, ensuring that clicking them updates the title correctly. I am also checking the UI for responsiveness and user experience.


**Refining Message UI**

I'm now refining the "Your Heart's Voice ❤️" section's UI in the component. I am focusing on improving the text area element for editing the `pageData.message`, ensuring it responds to updates and user input smoothly. I've integrated an "AI Polish" button, which I will test, and disabled it when the AI is loading. I am also checking the disabled status of the `Wand2` icon.


**Optimizing Message Component**

I'm now optimizing the "Your Heart's Voice" message section. I'm focusing on the user experience of the text area. I'm ensuring it responds smoothly to input. I also need to test the "AI Polish" button, making sure the UI works. I'm also ensuring the wand is disabled when loading.


**Integrating Milestone Logic**

I'm now integrating the milestone UI component with new functionality in the Builder, which is triggered if there is an empty timeline, or if explicitly requested. I've focused on ensuring the AI integration works as expected, and not modifying the existing `addTimelineEvent` unless necessary, avoiding potential conflicts.


**Analyzing Secret Surprise Logic**

I am now analyzing the conditional rendering of the "Secret Surprise" section within the component, specifically the logic based on `isPro` and `isSecret` states. I'm focusing on the implementation of the UI elements. I need to understand how the Lock and Sparkles icons are used, and ensuring both have proper usage. I also need to understand `unlock PRO` button.



**Refining Timeline Input**

I am now focusing on refining the timeline event input fields within the component. Specifically, I'm examining how the `title` and `description` of each event are handled. I'm verifying the input fields update the `pageData.timeline` state correctly using the `onChange` event, and ensuring seamless integration with the existing structure. I am also checking the error case.


**Reviewing Timeline Inputs**

I'm now carefully reviewing the timeline input fields, specifically how `description` is managed within the timeline array. I'm verifying the `onChange` handler, and ensuring proper state updates using `updatePage`. I'm also reviewing the delete functionality, and confirming its proper implementation.


**Analyzing Template Rendering**

I'm now carefully analyzing the template rendering logic within the component, specifically focusing on the conditional rendering of the template buttons. I need to understand how `isPro` and `t.isPro` interact with the buttons. I am checking the function. I also am checking the state, and am checking the `onClick` event.

I am also examining the `Lock` component, and its visibility based on `t.isPro` and `isPro`. I am also examining the current style, focusing on `border`, `bg`, and `text` styling.


**Analyzing Effect and GIF Logic**

I'm now analyzing the "Magical Effects" section, paying close attention to the `EFFECTS` array mapping. I am validating the correct state updates, focusing on the conditional rendering for `Lock` based on `isPro` and `e.isPro`. I'm also reviewing the "Cinematic GIF" section for its state management and rendering.


**Optimizing Tenor GIF Rendering**

I'm optimizing the "Cinematic GIF" section. I'm focusing on the rendering of the Tenor GIFs, specifically the use of `GIF_IDS` and conditional logic. I'm checking for proper state updates on click, ensuring seamless user experience. I'm also reviewing the implementation to ensure it's performant.


**Reviewing Publishing Section**

I'm now carefully reviewing the final "Review & Publish" section's UI. I'm focusing on the display of `pageData.title` and `pageData.day`, ensuring they render correctly within the component. I am reviewing the logic of `PRO` access. I also am ensuring that `Sparkles` properly renders.


**Evaluating UI Progress**

I am now closely examining the conditional rendering of the "Magical Effects" section, validating the `EFFECTS` mapping and its interaction with `isPro`. I'm verifying the rendering and the "Cinematic GIF" section as well, confirming its state management and how it renders.


**Refining Publication UI**

I am now focusing on refining the final publication section within the component, specifically the UI interactions and conditional rendering. I am carefully validating how `pageData.title` and `pageData.day` are rendered, ensuring accuracy. I'm reviewing the `PRO` access logic and making sure the `Sparkles` icon is displayed correctly. I'm verifying the UI flow.


**Enhancing AI Integration**

I am now focusing on refining the AI integration within the "Your Heart's Voice" section and timeline suggestions. I'm reviewing the `generateContent` function, ensuring it's robust and handles potential errors gracefully. I'm verifying that the error handling returns the original text if the AI fails, preventing disruptions in the user experience. I am also working on the timeline generation.


**Refining Schema Generation**

I'm now refining the JSON schema generation and parsing within the AI timeline generation. I've focused on ensuring the schema strictly enforces `title` and `description` within timeline events. I'm verifying the parsing of the AI response to handle potential errors and return an empty array, if necessary, to maintain a solid user experience and prevent interruptions.


**Validating Application State**

I'm now carefully examining the application's core state management, focusing on the `view`, `user`, `currentDay`, `activePage`, and `projects` states. I am verifying proper initialization, retrieval from localStorage, and the interactions of `handleLogin` and `handlePro`. I am checking updates to `localStorage`, and the application state.


**Validating Core State Management**

I am now thoroughly validating the core application state management, focusing on state interactions and updates to local storage, particularly `handleLogin` and `handlePro`. I am confirming proper initialization of view, user, currentDay, activePage, and projects. I've ensured a stable user experience.


**Streamlining Application State Logic**

I am now focusing on refining the application's core state management and its related functions. I'm scrutinizing `useEffect` usage and initial state values. I'm verifying data retrieval from `localStorage` and checking proper handling of updates to the `view`, `user`, and `projects` states. The goal is to make sure state is correct.


**Refining Builder Display**

payload.text:
You are now acting as a SENIOR PRODUCT ENGINEER whose job is to
AUDIT, FIX, OPTIMIZE, and HARDEN an already-built web app named “Lovia”.

This app is NOT a fresh build.
The UI, layout, animations already exist.
Design & motion are GOOD.
Your task is to FIX LOGIC, FEATURES, PERFORMANCE, and MONETIZATION.

DO NOT redesign the UI.
DO NOT change visual identity.
DO NOT simplify features.
ONLY FIX, COMPLETE, and OPTIMIZE.

====================================================
GLOBAL RULES (NON-NEGOTIABLE)
====================================================

1. Fix every issue listed below — no skipping.
2. Every button must WORK.
3. Every feature must be end-to-end functional.
4. Monetization rules must be strictly enforced.
5. No fake preview.
6. No placeholder logic.
7. No silent failures.
8. If a feature exists in UI, it MUST work.
9. AI speed must be optimized (no visible lag).
10. Code must remain production-ready.

====================================================
ISSUE FIX LIST (MANDATORY)
====================================================

----------------------------------------------------
1️⃣ AI TITLE GENERATOR (MISSING)
----------------------------------------------------

Problem:
- Title input has no AI assistance.

Fix:
- Add AI-powered Title Generator beside title input.
- Button label: “✨ Suggest Title”
- AI should generate:
  - Cute
  - Romantic
  - Emotional
  - Short & premium titles
- Based on:
  - Selected Valentine day
  - Message (if already written)

Rules:
- AI suggestion appears as preview
- User must click “Apply”
- AI NEVER auto-replaces text
- This feature is PRO ONLY

----------------------------------------------------
2️⃣ AI IS TOO SLOW
----------------------------------------------------

Problem:
- AI responses feel delayed and heavy.

Fix:
- Optimize AI calls:
  - Use short prompts
  - Stream response if supported
  - Disable blocking UI
- Show subtle loader:
  “Thinking with love… 💗”
- Cache last AI result per session
- Reduce payload size

Target:
- AI response visible within ~1–2 seconds

----------------------------------------------------
3️⃣ TIMELINE PHOTO FEATURE (MISSING)
----------------------------------------------------

Problem:
- Timeline cannot attach photos.

Fix:
- Add photo upload to each timeline milestone.

Access Rules:
- Free: max 3 timeline items (WITH or WITHOUT photo)
- Ad unlock: 1 ad → unlock 1 EXTRA timeline item WITH photo
- PRO: unlimited timeline items WITH photos

Rules:
- Photo optional per milestone
- Timeline preview must show photo elegantly
- Timeline animation remains soft & vertical
- Photo uploads must persist correctly

----------------------------------------------------
4️⃣ HIDDEN SURPRISE ACCESS LOGIC (BROKEN)
----------------------------------------------------

Problem:
- Hidden Surprise access rules unclear / broken.

Fix:
Access logic MUST be:

- Free users ❌ not allowed
- Watch 1 rewarded ad → unlock Hidden Surprise for 24h (per page)
- PRO → unlimited

UX:
- Locked state shows blur + lock icon
- On click → Upgrade popup:
  - 🎥 Watch 1 Ad (24h unlock)
  - 💎 Get Valentine PRO
  - Cancel

----------------------------------------------------
5️⃣ TEMPLATES SYSTEM IS BROKEN
----------------------------------------------------

Problems:
- Templates are not previewing
- All templates look the same
- No PRO / Ad distinction

Fix:
- Implement REAL preview (apply temporarily, revert on exit)
- Create STRONG visual difference:

FREE Templates:
- Simple layout
- Minimal animation
- Flat look

PRO Templates:
- Better spacing
- Depth layers
- Micro animations
- Premium typography
- Soft cinematic feel

Access Rules:
- Free: use free templates
- Ad unlock: 1 ad → unlock 1 PRO template for 24h
- PRO: all templates unlocked

----------------------------------------------------
6️⃣ MAGICAL EFFECTS SYSTEM (SAME ISSUE)
----------------------------------------------------

Problems:
- Effects feel identical
- Preview not clear
- Monetization not enforced

Fix:
- Each effect must look VISIBLY different
- Add live preview toggle
- Enforce rules:

Free:
- 7 basic effects

PRO:
- 8 premium effects

Ad unlock:
- 1 ad → unlock 1 premium effect for 24h

Limit:
- Max 3 effects active
- Auto-disable oldest if limit exceeded

----------------------------------------------------
7️⃣ CINEMATIC GIF SYSTEM IS BROKEN
----------------------------------------------------

Problems:
- GIF preview not working
- GIFs not visible on published love pages
- PRO / Ad logic broken

Fix:
Preview:
- Clicking GIF → preview animation plays ONCE

Render:
- GIF must appear on published love page
- Correct timing:
  - After message finishes
  - With fade + upward reveal

Access Rules:
- Free: ❌ cannot apply cinematic GIF
- Ad unlock:
  - 2 ads → unlock 1 GIF for 24h
- PRO: unlimited GIFs

Rules:
- Max 1 GIF per page
- Tenor script loaded ONCE
- GIF never loops aggressively

----------------------------------------------------
8️⃣ SHARE LINK & QR CODE (MISSING / BROKEN)
----------------------------------------------------

Problems:
- Share button not working
- No QR code generator

Fix:
Share Button:
- Must copy public URL
- Must open native share where possible

QR Code:
- Auto-generate on publish
- Cute framed design
- Text: “Scan to feel my love 💗”

Access:
- View QR: free
- Download QR image: PRO only

----------------------------------------------------
9️⃣ BROKEN BUTTONS
----------------------------------------------------

Problem:
- Some buttons do nothing.

Fix:
- Audit ALL buttons:
  - Create
  - Continue
  - Preview
  - Publish
  - Add Timeline
  - Add Surprise
  - Apply Template
  - Apply Effect
  - Upgrade
  - Watch Ad
- Every button must have:
  - Click handler
  - Success feedback
  - Error handling

NO dead buttons allowed.

----------------------------------------------------
🔟 AI FEATURES ACCESS CONTROL
----------------------------------------------------

Fix:
- ALL AI features must be PRO ONLY:
  - AI message helper
  - AI polish
  - AI title generator
  - AI emotion detection
  - AI gift recommendation
  - AI animation timing

Free users:
- See AI buttons (locked)
- Clicking shows upgrade popup

====================================================
FINAL VALIDATION CHECK (MANDATORY)
====================================================

Before completing fixes, verify:

- Every feature works without refresh
- State persists after refresh
- Published pages load perfectly
- Monetization rules cannot be bypassed
- No console errors
- Mobile-first UX preserved
- No performance regression

Only adjust logic, handlers, and missing parts.

**Analyzing Lovia's Builder**

I'm digging into `Builder.tsx`, the heart of Lovia's Valentine page builder. I'm focusing on the core logic: the construction of the page. My priorities are to evaluate and repair its efficiency, and explore options for enhancing both performance and how the product is monetized. I need to understand its limitations for optimization.


**Addressing Missing Features**

I've pinpointed several critical missing features. The AI Title Generator is a priority, to be a PRO-only addition beside the title input. I'm also addressing AI performance by optimizing prompts, preventing UI blocking, and adding a subtle loader. I'm adding photo uploads to milestones, with free users limited to three and tiered access for Ads and PRO. The broken "Hidden Surprise Logic" requires attention, with the free version locked, and Ad/PRO access also tiered. I also need to fix the templates, effects, and GIF systems, with ad/pro restrictions. I'll need to sort out sharing and QR code functionality, too, probably in the preview.


**Implementing Builder Features**

I'm focused on implementing new features in `Builder.tsx`. I'll begin with adding an AI Title Suggestion button to Step 1, restricted to PRO users. Next, I will address the AI helper logic and performance in Step 2, and then move on to adding photo upload functionality in Step 3 (Timeline), enforcing usage limits. Finally, I will tackle Step 4 (Templates/Effects/GIFs), to implement access control restrictions.


**Prioritizing File Fixes**

I am now circling back to prioritize the initial file fixes, specifically focusing on `Builder.tsx`, as I understand this addresses Templates, Effects, and GIF systems. My primary goal right now is to tackle Step 4's access control restrictions, and the GIF preview. I have updated the AI call, and I will be looking to get the suggested Title running next.

payload.text:
**Diagnosing the Missing Export**

I'm currently focused on a compilation error within `components/Builder.tsx`. The error points to a missing export: `getTitleSuggestions` is not available in `services/geminiService`. Inspection confirms this; the function is absent in the target file, necessitating a closer look at the service definition.


**Implementing the Missing Function**

I've confirmed the compilation error is due to the missing `getTitleSuggestions` export in `services/geminiService.ts`, as suspected. I need to implement this now. I also double-checked the `@google/genai` usage, and it seems I've followed the guidelines already, so I am good to proceed with the function implementation.


**Adding Message Suggestions**

I've been working on integrating AI-powered message suggestions into the application. I've implemented `getAIPolish`, to rewrite a message to match the specified mood. Now, I'm working on `getMessageSuggestions`, which generates new message ideas, also by leveraging the AI, tailored to the user's "pro" status. This function constructs a prompt that adapts based on the "pro" setting.


**Generating Valentine's Day Prompts**

I'm currently fleshing out `getMessageSuggestions`. I'm configuring the AI model with specific prompts for Valentine's Day, focusing on generating three unique message starters for young lovers. I'm taking into account the specified mood and "pro" status of the user, keeping the generated suggestions concise and under 60 words. I am also working on `generateTimelineSuggestions` and have defined the schema for the AI response.


**Defining Title Generation Logic**

I've just added the `getTitleSuggestions` function to `services/geminiService.ts`. I'm using the Gemini model to generate concise, romantic titles based on a provided day and message context. The function structures the prompt to get 4 suggestions, keeping them catchy and sweet. The response is parsed to ensure data integrity and proper handling of potential errors. I've also implemented error handling.


**Verifying Component Imports**

I'm now cross-referencing imports in `Builder.tsx`. The specific export error is confirmed. I've rechecked the imports on line 23 of `Builder.tsx`, the function `getTitleSuggestions` on line 108 of `Builder.tsx`, and the `getMessageSuggestions` on line 133 of `Builder.tsx`. All imports and function usages check out. Now, I'm verifying the `onUpgrade` prop in relation to `monetizationModal`.

entries.description: Added the missing getTitleSuggestions export to generate romantic title ideas using Gemini API.

I'm exploring external QR code APIs like `https://api.qrserver.com/v1/create-qr-code/` for simplicity and avoiding external dependencies, since I don't know the stability of those dependencies. I'm aiming for straightforward implementation and considering separate logic for a PRO download of the generated QR code.

entries.description: Hardened the Preview component with real Template rendering logic, advanced Particle Effects system, cinematic GIF reveals, and the Share/QR Code system (Issue 8). Implemented PRO-gated QR downloads and line-by-line emotional storytelling.
